title: tristellateia grandiflora
intro: Tristellateia grandiflora, also known as the Hawaiian hibiscus or Hawaiian rosemallow, is a flowering plant native to Hawaii. It has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Tristellateia grandiflora can be used as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in skincare products due to its antioxidant and soothing properties.
